statement: Council tool routes always win: a loadout server (programmatic or TELOS_LOADOUT file) can never shadow a seat server or a council tool route — registry servers spread last in withLoadout, and tool routes are untouched by it.

oracle.contract_equals_code: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (probe: withLoadout attempts to shadow the ai-peer server; the registry must win)

statement: claude_ask and agy_checkpoint route to the in-repo ai-peer-mcp server, never a plugin — the agy APPROVAL seat is local governance derived from the dossier and must not become a model call (ask_agy is a separate ADVISORY seat).

oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-seat-registry.mjs ('the agy APPROVAL seat is local governance — it must not become a model call')

statement: An unrouted tool throws BEFORE any backend spawn; a namespaced call to an unregistered server fails closed. No seat call silently lands on the wrong process.

oracle.test: breakout/scripts/test-seat-router.mjs (no backend is contacted for an unrouted tool)

statement: A build that needs capabilities it does not have must surface them: a missing required capability packet blocks, missing_capabilities not presented to claude blocks, and an open must_request_user_or_install item blocks until resolved.

oracle.test: build-gate/scripts/test-gate.mjs + test-trust.mjs (capability paths); build-gate/examples/capability-blocked/ (worked fail-closed fixture)

statement: Every SPECIFIED-PENDING slice carries a per-task loadout review record (TASK-LOADOUTS/task-<id>.json) naming its entry ritual, the oracles it will flip, what to reuse, and its optimization opportunities — reviewed at slice start, before code.
